name: Adobe Experience Manager (AEM) Content API
what_it_does: Enterprise-grade Digital Experience Platform with REST/GraphQL API — enabling large organizations to manage content, digital assets (DAM), personalize experiences via Adobe Target integration, and deliver headless content via AEM Content Fragments and GraphQL, with deep integration across Adobe Experience Cloud (Analytics, Target, Campaign, Marketo) for unified customer experience orchestration.
use_cases: Delivering headless content via AEM Content Fragments GraphQL API for Next.js/React frontends
Managing digital assets (images, video, documents) via AEM Assets REST API for DAM integration
Integrating AEM content with Adobe Target for AI-powered personalization via Experience Cloud
Publishing content workflows via AEM REST API for content operation automation
Querying AEM content for omnichannel delivery (web, mobile, IoT) via GraphQL persisted queries
not_for: Small and mid-market companies (AEM is among the most expensive and complex enterprise CMS)
Organizations without Adobe Experience Cloud investment (AEM value multiplies within Adobe stack)
Rapid deployment (AEM implementation is typically 12-24+ months for full deployment)
best_when: A Fortune 500 company already invested in Adobe Experience Cloud (Analytics, Target, Campaign, Marketo) needs content management that natively integrates with their Adobe stack — especially in financial services, healthcare, retail, and media.
avoid_when: You're not an enterprise, don't have Adobe Experience Cloud, need rapid deployment, have limited IT budget, or prefer open-source foundations.

auth.notes: Adobe IMS (Identity Management System) OAuth2 for API access. Bearer token auth. Organization-scoped with product profile-based access. Webhook for content and asset events. GraphQL persisted queries for headless delivery. AEM Assets REST API for DAM operations. Adobe Developer Console for API client registration. Java SDK for AEM development. JavaScript SDK for AEM headless. SAML SSO via Adobe IMS (industry-standard enterprise identity). Service Account credentials for system integrations.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier; enterprise pricing only
pricing.paid_tiers: AEM Sites + Assets from $250,000+/year; contact Adobe Enterprise sales; typically bundled with Adobe Experience Cloud
pricing.notes: Adobe (San Jose, CA). Founded 1982. NASDAQ listed (ADBE). $19B+ revenue (FY2023). Adobe Experience Manager traces to CQ5 (acquired from Day Software, 2010). Adobe Experience Cloud includes Analytics, Target, Campaign, Marketo, and more. AEM is the cornerstone of Adobe's enterprise DXP strategy. Used by BMW, Marriott, Best Buy, T-Mobile, Sony. Gartner Magic Quadrant Leader for DXP. Adobe Sensei AI for automated content tagging. AEM as a Cloud Service (managed) vs. on-premises/managed options. $20B Figma acquisition (attempted, blocked by EU regulators, 2023).

performance.rate_limits: Enterprise rate limits per contract; CDN-delivered persisted queries at edge speed

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: GraphQL persisted queries + REST API. Adobe IMS OAuth2. Extensive enterprise documentation via developer.adobe.com. Java + JavaScript SDK. Adobe Developer Console for API registration. AEM as Cloud Service vs. on-premises have different deployment patterns. Adobe Experience Cloud integration requires multiple product configurations. Significant implementation partner ecosystem for enterprise deployment.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Content reads fully idempotent. Asset reads idempotent by UUID. GraphQL persisted queries idempotent. Content mutations use JCR node path for idempotency.
known_agent_gotchas: Adobe IMS OAuth2 — requires Adobe Developer Console registration and service account setup
Implementation partner (SI) required — 12-24+ month enterprise implementation
AEM as Cloud Service (SaaS) and on-premises have different API patterns — verify deployment
GraphQL persisted queries must be pre-registered — cannot use arbitrary GraphQL in production
Enterprise pricing ($250K+/year) and complexity make evaluation expensive without commitment

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR, HIPAA, PCI-DSS, FedRAMP compliant. Multi-region global data residency. Adobe IMS OAuth2 with product profile scopes. NASDAQ-listed Adobe with enterprise security standards. DAM assets may include confidential creative content — strict access controls required. HIPAA BAA for healthcare. FedRAMP authorization for US government. Adobe Sensei AI content processing — review data handling for sensitive creative content. Service Account credentials must be rotated and stored securely.
